content: The differing intrinsic reward scaling of curiosity-driven methods (ICM overestimates novelty of high-dimensional random noise, while RND regularizes this bias) can be repurposed as a dynamic pruning metric for active learning agent episodic memory to retain only high-value, learnable novel states for long-horizon planning. This creates a self-sustaining memory loop where intrinsic curiosity signals automatically curate memory without extra supervised annotation costs.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
